page: Amazon Web Services c8gb.medium is a compute-optimized instance powered by a dedicated 2.8 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor built on the ARMv9 arm64 architecture. This server features 1 physical core with 1 thread, 2.0 GB of memory, and no local storage. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a split in performance capabilities. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression both rank in the average tier, falling into the middle 50%. Notably, single-threaded bzip3 decompression shows strong performance, placing in the top 25%. However, due to its single-core configuration, multi-core benchmarks like stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores fall into the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with large-block read and write speeds ranking in the bottom 25% and bottom 10% respectively.

The primary tradeoff of the c8gb.medium is its single-core limitation and low memory capacity, which restrict its utility for parallel processing or memory-intensive applications. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for lightweight, single-threaded workloads that benefit from dedicated ARMv9 architecture without requiring high memory density. Appropriate workloads include basic single-threaded scripting, lightweight web serving, simple database operations, and minor text or compression tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.medium is a compute-optimized instance featuring a single dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 core running at 2.8 GHz, paired with 2.0 GB of memory and no local storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities, with single-threaded gzip compression in the middle 50% and bzip3 decompression reaching the top 25%. However, the single-core architecture limits multi-threaded performance, placing multi-core stress-ng and PassMark CPU scores in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with large-block write speeds in the bottom 10%. This instance represents a low-resource, entry-level compute option, qualitatively cost-efficient for lightweight, single-threaded tasks. It is best suited for basic scripting, simple web serving, and minor data compression workloads that do not require multi-core scaling or high memory capacity.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.medium is a compute-optimized ARMv9 instance with 1 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 core, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth, suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks.

tagline: A single-core ARMv9 instance designed for lightweight, single-threaded workloads requiring dedicated compute resources with a minimal memory footprint.
bullet_points: Single dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 core at 2.8 GHz
2.0 GB of system memory and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression performance in the top 25%
Average single-core stress-ng and gzip compression performance
Constrained multi-core and memory bandwidth performance due to single-core design
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded scripting and basic web serving
